summary: Aggregates a filtered USAspending search population into time buckets such as fiscal years, calendar years, quarters, or months.
businessPurpose: Use this endpoint when the analytical question is about how spending changes over time for a filtered set of awards, transactions, or subawards, not when the question needs individual award records.
analyticalGrain: One row is one time bucket for the filtered population and selected spending_level. It is an aggregate time series, not an award, recipient, agency, or transaction row.

primaryEntities.name: Filtered spending population
primaryEntities.description: The awards, transactions, or subawards selected by the AdvancedFilterObject before aggregation.

primaryEntities.name: Time bucket
primaryEntities.description: The fiscal year, calendar year, quarter, or month returned in each result row.

measures.name: aggregated_amount
measures.description: Total obligation-like amount for the bucket and selected spending level.

measures.name: Award category obligations
measures.description: Category-specific obligation columns such as Contract_Obligations, Grant_Obligations, Direct_Obligations, Idv_Obligations, Loan_Obligations, and Other_Obligations.

measures.name: Outlays
measures.description: Nullable total and category outlay columns returned alongside obligations.

dimensions.name: group
dimensions.description: The time grouping: fiscal_year, calendar_year, quarter, or month. Live validation also accepts aliases.

dimensions.name: spending_level
dimensions.description: The data source level for the aggregate: transactions, awards, or subawards.

dimensions.name: Advanced filters
dimensions.description: Search filters such as time period, award type codes, keywords, agencies, recipients, TAS, PSC, NAICS, program activities, and disaster codes.

suitableQuestions.name: How did contract obligations trend by fiscal year?
suitableQuestions.description: Use group=fiscal_year and award_type_codes for procurement awards.

suitableQuestions.name: What is the quarterly subaward trend for a bounded period?
Does a keyword search show a broad spending trend?
suitableQuestions.description: Keyword-only requests work but should be bounded for repeatable analysis.

notSuitableFor.name: Listing individual awards
notSuitableFor.description: This endpoint returns aggregate time buckets. Use spending_by_award for award-level rows.

notSuitableFor.name: Historical pre-2007 search analysis
notSuitableFor.description: Live responses warn that search time periods are limited to an earliest date of 2007-10-01.

joins.description: Reuse a compatible filters object to move from aggregate trend to representative award-level records.

workflows.name: Trend then inspect awards
workflows.description: First aggregate spending over time, then reuse the same filters against spending_by_award to inspect the rows driving a period.

steps.action: Call spending_over_time with a bounded filters.time_period and the desired group.
Identify periods with unusually high or low aggregated_amount.
Reuse the filters on spending_by_award and add fields/sort/page controls to retrieve award-level rows.

caveats.statement: Use canonical group names in generated requests even though live validation accepts aliases.
caveats.impact: This avoids encoding undocumented UI aliases as the preferred public MCP surface.

caveats.statement: Prefer spending_level='subawards' over the legacy subawards boolean.
caveats.impact: The live API returns a deprecation warning for the subawards field.
